title: Warp Commerce Model — Party (Primitive 1)
description: A Party is any entity that can participate in commerce. Parties hold value, make commitments, fulfill obligations, and act as intermediaries or guarantors. Role is contextual, not intrinsic.

PartyID.description: Branded string identifier (brand: PartyID). Globally unique, immutable, never reused (Invariant 5). JSON Schema cannot enforce the brand; the TypeScript generator re-applies it as `string & { __brand: 'PartyID' }`.

PartyType.description: Individual = a natural person; Organization = a legal entity (company, NGO, government); System = an AI agent or automated system acting on behalf of a principal party.

PartyRole.description: The role a Party plays in a Commitment. Contextual, not intrinsic — the same Party can be Initiator in one Commitment and Counterparty in another.

PartyLocale.description: Locale of a Party: language (BCP 47), currency (ISO 4217), jurisdiction (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2).

PartyCapacity.description: What a Party is verified able to do (Invariant 3: Capacity Verification). The safe default is everything false until verified.

verified_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp at which capacity was verified.
